data.name: Tutorial_Welcome_Content
data.value: Beutl is a cross-platform video editor. This tutorial will guide you through the basics.

data.name: Tutorial_Welcome_MenuBar_Content
data.value: The menu bar contains options for file management, editing, and more.

data.name: Tutorial_Welcome_Create_Content
data.value: Click here to create a new project or scene to start editing.

data.name: Tutorial_SceneEdit_Description
data.value: Learn to add elements, edit properties, and create animations

data.name: Tutorial_SceneEdit_Step1_Content
data.value: Right-click on the timeline and add an ellipse element. You can also drag and drop from the library.

data.name: Tutorial_SceneEdit_Step2_Content
data.value: Click on the element you just added in the timeline to select it.

data.name: Tutorial_SceneEdit_Step3_Content
data.value: This is the properties tab. You can view and edit the properties of the selected element here.

data.name: Tutorial_SceneEdit_Step4_Content
data.value: Find the Width property and click the menu icon next to it to enable animation. You can enable it with "Edit animation" or "Edit animation in inline view"

data.name: Tutorial_SceneEdit_Step5_Content
data.value: The playback position has been moved forward. Click the diamond icon to add a keyframe at this position.

data.name: Tutorial_SceneEdit_Step6_Content
data.value: The Width value has been changed to 300. This creates an animation from 100 to 300 over time.

data.name: Tutorial_SceneEdit_Step7_Content
data.value: Press the Play button in the player to preview your animation.

data.name: Tutorial_SceneEdit_Step8_Content
data.value: Congratulations! You have learned how to add elements, edit properties, and create animations. Explore more features on your own!

data.name: Tutorial_AnimationEdit_Description
data.value: Learn advanced animation editing using the graph editor

data.name: Tutorial_AnimationEdit_Step1_Content
data.value: Select the element you want to animate in the timeline.

data.name: Tutorial_AnimationEdit_Step2_Content
data.value: Click the + button on the Transform property and add "Translate".

data.name: Tutorial_AnimationEdit_Step3_Content
data.value: Click the menu icon on the X property and select "Edit animation".

data.name: Tutorial_AnimationEdit_Step4_Content
data.value: The playback position has been moved forward. Click the diamond icon to add a keyframe at this position.

data.name: Tutorial_AnimationEdit_Step5_Content
data.value: Dragging an easing function from the Library panel (Easing tab) and dropping it onto the Graph Editor adds keyframes using that easing function. Dropping it at the same position as an existing keyframe changes the easing function of that keyframe.

data.name: Tutorial_AnimationEdit_Step6_Content
data.value: In the graph editor, round icons represent easing control points. Move them left or right to reveal the diamond icons (keyframes) below.

data.name: Tutorial_AnimationEdit_Step7_Content
data.value: You can drag diamond icons to change the time and value of keyframes.

data.name: Tutorial_AnimationEdit_Step8_Content
data.value: Press the Play button to preview your animation.

data.name: Tutorial_AnimationEdit_Step9_Content
data.value: Right-click on the graph editor background and select "Copy All" to copy all keyframes.

data.name: Tutorial_AnimationEdit_Step10_Content
data.value: Click the menu icon on the Y property and select "Edit animation".

data.name: Tutorial_AnimationEdit_Step11_Content
data.value: Right-click on the Y property's graph editor and select "Paste" to apply the copied animation.

data.name: Tutorial_AnimationEdit_Step12_Content
data.value: Press the Play button again to verify that X and Y animations work together.

data.name: Tutorial_AnimationEdit_Step13_Content
data.value: You have learned advanced animation editing using the graph editor, applying easing functions, and copying/pasting animations. Explore more features on your own!
